hypothesis: On QM9 with the MPEU model at batch size 32 on a single NVIDIA A100, a static-64 training run preceded by a warmup that executes gradient updates on synthesized batches covering the 10 most frequent multiples-of-64 padding sizes estimated from a 1,000-graph pilot sample will reduce recompilations by at least 90% and lower the mean combined time per step over the first 100,000 steps by at least 10% relative to standard dynamic batching, without a statistically significant change in final test RMSE (p≥0.10).
